content: Self-Refine’s task-focused iterative output polishing and Reflexion’s dynamic memory-backed agent self-reflection can be combined to create a two-layer self-improvement system for LLMs: Self-Refine handles surface-level task output refinement in the short term, while Reflexion accumulates cross-task reflective insights into structured memory to rewrite the LLM’s implicit task heuristics over the long term. This hybrid framework retains the core advantage of both approaches—requiring no extra supervised training or reinforcement learning—while closing the gap between single-task polishing and cumulative agent improvement.
